summary: sendgrid-sdk: typosquat with install-time beacon to hardcoded IP
details: The package impersonates the official Twilio SendGrid SDK (published under the @sendgrid/* scope) but ships no SendGrid functionality — index.js exports an empty object. Its sole effect is a postinstall lifecycle script that issues a plain-HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare IP (46.224.67.169:3000/ping), carrying the package name and version, the installer's CI environment variable, and the installer's Node.js version. This fires unconditionally on `npm install`, leaking installer environment characteristics to an attacker-controlled host and confirming the machine as a reachable target. The name-confusion vector (against @sendgrid/mail and related @sendgrid/* packages) is the recruitment mechanism for the beacon. Although the package's description self-labels as a 'honeypot,' the install-time exfiltration to a non-publisher bare-IP endpoint over cleartext HTTP harms any developer or build system that installs it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
